label: PASS
rationale: Admin SP Anybody has ResponseSign Null/HashAndSign None, so a successful SyncSession without SignedHash is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: SignedHash, if present, is tied to an SPSigningAuthority response signing credential; Admin SP Anybody preconfigures ResponseSign as Null.

label: PASS
rationale: SID is preconfigured with ResponseSign Null and HashAndSign None; omitting SignedHash in the successful SyncSession is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: SID password startup does not create an SP Signing Authority because SID ResponseSign is Null, so returning SignedHash is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory observes SID ResponseSign as Null; the later successful SyncSession correctly omits SignedHash.

label: FAIL
rationale: After observing ResponseSign Null for SID, there is no SPSigningAuthority credential to sign SyncSession; a SignedHash return is invalid.
